async_execution.mechanism: Factory Droid hooks run synchronously; no async execution documented

context_injection.mechanism: Factory Droid hooks cannot inject context into the agent session

decision_control.mechanism: hook_exit_code_behavior: Factory Droid hooks use exit codes to signal block (non-zero) or allow (zero) decisions on the triggering action

handler_types.mechanism: Factory Droid hooks execute shell commands only; no other handler types documented

hook_scopes.mechanism: Factory Droid hooks are project-scoped; no multi-scope configuration documented

input_modification.mechanism: Factory Droid hooks cannot modify tool input before execution

json_io_protocol.mechanism: Factory Droid hooks communicate via exit codes; no JSON stdin/stdout protocol documented

matcher_patterns.mechanism: Factory Droid hooks fire on configured lifecycle events; no per-tool pattern matching documented

permission_control.mechanism: Factory Droid hooks cannot make permission decisions about tool availability

providerExtensions.name: Hook Events
providerExtensions.description: Eight hook events: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Notification, UserPromptSubmit, Stop, SubagentStop, PreCompact, SessionStart, SessionEnd. PreToolUse can block tool execution (decision: block). PostToolUse can trigger rerun (decision: rerun). UserPromptSubmit can modify inputText before processing.

providerExtensions.name: Hook Config Structure
providerExtensions.description: Hooks are configured as JSON under a top-level "hooks" key: {"hooks": {"EventName": [{"matcher": "ToolPattern", "hooks": [{"type": "command", "command": "..."}]}]}}. Matchers support exact strings, regex patterns, and "*" for all tools. MCP tool names follow the mcp__serverName__toolName pattern.

providerExtensions.name: Hook Scope Files
providerExtensions.description: Hooks are stored in scoped settings files: ~/.factory/settings.json (user-global), .factory/settings.json (project, committed), .factory/settings.local.json (project-local, not committed). Plugin hooks live in plugins/[name]/settings.json.

providerExtensions.name: Hook Exit Code Behavior
providerExtensions.description: Exit code 2 has special semantics: suppress output and return empty result. JSON output fields include decision, reason, stopReason, inputText, and suppressOutput. The FACTORY_TOOL_INPUT environment variable is available to hook processes.

providerExtensions.name: PreCompact Hook Event
providerExtensions.description: The PreCompact event fires before context compaction occurs, enabling hooks to inject memory or state into the compaction prompt. Useful for preserving important session context across compaction boundaries.
